my_thoughts: The denial/consequence works: no negotiation (cannot check), asserts: consequence = zero result (refusal = no work). The student questions term (seeking to negotiate cost), which shows they understand: price = negotiable (negotiation). They'll likely ask about mechanism next. Hypothesis: negotiation denials trigger mechanism questions—testable by tracking whether negotiation denials lead to mechanism inquiries.
accumulated_insight: T1: Student questions term ('There is no check on the price? You cannot find out whether it is too high?')—seeking to negotiate cost via questions term and seeks negotiation. They understand: price = negotiable (negotiation). The comparison_contrast mechanism denies: no negotiation (cannot check), asserts: consequence = zero result (refusal = no work). Pattern: negotiation question → denial/consequence → [next: mechanism question]. Low confidence because negotiation denials can lead to clarification requests or resistance.
visible_response: Some people think it is too high, refuse to pay and get nothing.
